nameMeanings.name: Einion
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Welsh: Anglicized form of Welsh ap Einion, ap Eynon ‘son of Einion’. See Eynon and compare Bennion . This form of the surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Eynon
meanings: Welsh: from Enniaun, an old Welsh personal name ultimately derived from Late Latin Annianus, but also associated with Welsh einion ‘anvil’, taken as implying stability and fortitude. See also Beynon and compare Onion .

nameMeanings.name: Philip
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the Greek name Philippos, meaning ‘lover of horses’, from philein ‘to love’ + hippos ‘horse’. This was popular in the classical period and since. It was the name of the father of Alexander the Great . It was also the name of one of Christ's apostles, of a deacon ordained by the apostles after the death of Christ, and of several other early saints. See also Philippa .

nameMeanings.name: ap
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of any of the names beginning with this syllable.

variants.name: Alexander
meanings: From the Latin form of the Greek name Alexandros, from alexein ‘to defend’ + anēr ‘man, warrior’ (genitive andros). The name became extremely popular in the post-classical period, and was borne by several individuals in the New Testament and some early Christian saints. Its use as a common given name throughout Europe, however, derives largely from the fame of Alexander the Great , King of Macedon ( 356–323 bc ), around whom a large body of popular legend grew up in late antiquity, much of which came to be embodied in the medieval ‘Alexander romances’.

variants.name: Alan
meanings: Of Celtic origin and uncertain derivation (possibly a diminutive of a word meaning ‘rock’). It was introduced into England by Breton followers of William the Conqueror , most notably Alan , Earl of Brittany, who was rewarded for his services with vast estates in the newly conquered kingdom. In Britain the variants Allan and Allen are considerably less frequent, and generally represent transferred uses of surname forms, whereas in America all three forms of the name are approximately equally common. See also Alun .

variants.name: Albert
meanings: From an Old French name, Albert, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from adal ‘noble’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to England, displacing the Old English form Æþelbeorht. The name is popular in a variety of forms in Western Europe, and has been traditional in a number of European princely families. It was out of favour in England for centuries, however, and the revival of its popularity in the 19th century was largely in honour of Queen Victoria's consort, Prince Albert of Saxe-Coburg-Gotha .

en.lifeSummary: Philip ap Einion of Faerdre was born about 1266, in Pencarreg, Carmarthenshire, Wales as the son of Einion ap Gruffudd and Ellyw II ferch Maredudd. He had at least 3 sons with Efa ferch Ieuan.

fr.lifeSummary: Philip ap Einion of Faerdre est né environ 1266, à Pencarreg, Carmarthenshire, Galles, fils d’Einion ap Gruffudd et Ellyw II ferch Maredudd. Il a eu au moins 3 fils avec Efa ferch Ieuan.

de.lifeSummary: Philip ap Einion of Faerdre wurde um 1266 in Pencarreg, Carmarthenshire, Wales, als Sohn von Einion ap Gruffudd und Ellyw II ferch Maredudd geboren. Er hatte mit Efa ferch Ieuan mindestens 3 Söhne.

it.lifeSummary: Philip ap Einion of Faerdre è nato il circa 1266 nel Pencarreg, Carmarthenshire, Galles come figlio di Einion ap Gruffudd e Ellyw II ferch Maredudd. Lui avuto almeno 3 figli con Efa ferch Ieuan.

ja.lifeSummary: Philip ap Einion of Faerdreは約1266年にウェールズカーマースエンシュアイアペンカーレグでEinion ap GruffuddとEllyw II ferch Mareduddの息子として生まれた。彼はEfa ferch Ieuanとの間に少なくとも3人の息子をもうけた。

ko.lifeSummary: Philip ap Einion of Faerdre(은)는 대략 1266년 웨일스 카마테실르 펜카르그에서 Einion ap Gruffudd(와)과 Ellyw II ferch Maredudd의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 Efa ferch Ieuan(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Philip ap Einion of Faerdre nasceu aproximadamente 1266, em Pencarreg, Carmarthenshire, Gales filho de Einion ap Gruffudd e Ellyw II ferch Maredudd. Ele teve pelo menos 3 filhos com Efa ferch Ieuan.

ru.lifeSummary: Philip ap Einion of Faerdre родился около 1266 г. в местности пенкаррег, кармартеншир, Уэльс. Его родителей звали Einion ap Gruffudd и Ellyw II ferch Maredudd. У него было по крайней мере 3 сына от Efa ferch Ieuan.

es.lifeSummary: Philip ap Einion of Faerdre nació aproximadamente 1266, en Pencarreg, Carmarthenshire, Gales como hijo de Einion ap Gruffudd y Ellyw II ferch Maredudd. Tuvo por lo menos 3 hijos con Efa ferch Ieuan.
